channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: CR4T: Rewrite-Based Guardrails for Adolescent LLM Safety
item.description: arXiv:2605.21609v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in adolescent digital environments, mediating information seeking, advice, and emotionally sensitive interactions. Yet existing safety mechanisms remain largely grounded in adult-centric norms and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, LLM, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Broadening Access to Transportation Safety Data with Generative AI: A Schema-Grounded Framework for Spatial Natural Language Queries
item.description: arXiv:2605.21712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transportation safety analysis requires integrating crash records, roadway attributes, and geospatial data through GIS-based workflows, but access remains uneven across agencies and community stakeholders. Technical prerequisites create a gap between ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, paper, LLM, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sem-Detect: Semantic Level Detection of AI Generated Peer-Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2605.21713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can we distinguish whether a peer review was written by a human or generated by an AI model? We argue that, in this setting, authorship should not be attributed solely from the textual features of a review, but also from the ideas, judgments, and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, paper, analysis, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Cases Get Rare: A Retrieval Benchmark for Off-Guideline Clinical Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2605.21807v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Across medical specialties, clinical practice is anchored in evidence-based guidelines that codify best studied diagnostic and treatment pathways. These pathways routinely fall short for the long tail of real-world care not covered by guidelines. Most...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, context, arxiv, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Does Slightly Mean Somewhat? Measuring Vague Intensity Words in LLM Numeric Actions
item.description: arXiv:2605.21827v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Do language models preserve the ordinal meaning of intensity words when those words must produce numeric actions? I study a researcher-constructed scale of 10 English degree modifiers, from slightly to drastically, informed by the Quirk et al. degree-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, LLM, study, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OSCToM: RL-Guided Adversarial Generation for High-Order Theory of Mind
item.description: arXiv:2605.20423v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform well on many language tasks, but their Theory of Mind (ToM) reasoning is still uneven in complex social settings. Existing benchmarks, including ExploreToM, do not always test the recursive beliefs and information ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, paper, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: High Quality Embeddings for Horn Logic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.20467v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural networks can be trained to rank the choices made by logical reasoners, resulting in more efficient searches for answers. A key step in this process is creating useful embeddings, i.e., numeric representations of logical statements. This paper i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, knowledge base, paper, experiment, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mahjax: A GPU-Accelerated Mahjong Simulator for Reinforcement Learning in JAX
item.description: arXiv:2605.20577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Riichi Mahjong is a multi-player, imperfect-information game characterized by stochasticity and high-dimensional state spaces. These attributes present a unique combination of challenges that mirror complex real-world decision-making problems in reinf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vector, experiment, research, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Temporal Contrastive Transformer for Financial Crime Detection: Self-Supervised Sequence Embeddings via Predictive Contrastive Coding
item.description: arXiv:2605.21490v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the Temporal Contrastive Transformer (TCT), a representation learning framework designed to capture contextual temporal dynamics in sequences of financial transactions. The model is trained using a self-supervised contrastive objective to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, product, experiment, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Teaching Language Models to Forecast Research Success Through Comparative Idea Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2605.21491v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As language models accelerate scientific research by automating hypothesis generation and implementation, a new bottleneck emerges: evaluating and filtering hundreds of AI-generated ideas without exhaustive experimentation. We ask whether LMs can lear...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, study, experiment, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Harnesses for Inference-Time Alignment over Execution Trajectories
item.description: arXiv:2605.21516v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Harness engineering has emerged as an important inference-time technique for large language model (LLM) agents, aiming to improve long-term performance through task decomposition and guided execution. However, more elaborate harnesses are not uniforml...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, arxiv, LLM, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DualOptim+: Bridging Shared and Decoupled Optimizer States for Better Machine Unlearning in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.21539v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose DualOptim+, a novel optimization framework for improving machine unlearning in large language models. It introduces a base state to capture common representations shared by forgetting and retaining objectives and delta states to preserve ob...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, alignment, arxiv, experiment, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Open-World Evaluations for Measuring Frontier AI Capabilities
item.description: arXiv:2605.20520v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Benchmark-based evaluation remains important for tracking frontier AI progress. But it can both overstate and understate deployed capability because it privileges tasks that can be precisely specified, automatically graded, easy to optimize for, and r...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, paper, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Reproducible Log-Driven AutoML Framework for Interpretable Pipeline Optimization in Healthcare Risk Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2605.21528v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate and reproducible disease risk prediction remains challenging due to heterogeneous features, limited samples, and severe class imbalance. This study introduces yvsoucom-iterkit, a deterministic and log-driven automated machine learning framewo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, experiment, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RankJudge: A Multi-Turn LLM-as-a-Judge Synthetic Benchmark Generator
item.description: arXiv:2605.21748v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As interactive LLM-based applications are created and refined, model developers need to evaluate the quality of generated text along many possible axes. For simpler systems, human evaluation may be practical, but in complicated systems like conversati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Personality Engineering with AI Agents: A New Methodology for Negotiation Research
item.description: arXiv:2605.20554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: According to canonical negotiation theory, people's success in a negotiation depends on how well they balance competing demands--empathizing and asserting, demonstrating concern for other and concern for self, being soft on the people and hard on the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, ICL, experiment, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Double descent for least-squares interpolation on contaminated data: A simulation study
item.description: arXiv:2605.21494v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Overparametrized models can exhibit an excellent generalization performance, although they should be prone to overfitting according to classical statistical theory. The discovery of the "double descent", indicating that the generalization error decrea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
